content.problemStatement:
You are given two points PPP and QQQ and an opaque sphere in a three-dimensional space. The point PPP is not moving, while QQQ is moving in a straight line with constant velocity. You are also given a direction vector ddd with the following meaning: the position of QQQ at time ttt is Q(t)=Q(0)+d⋅tQ(t) = Q(0) + d \cdot tQ(t)=Q(0)+d⋅t, where Q(0)Q(0)Q(0) is the initial position of QQQ.

It is guaranteed that QQQ is not visible from PPP initially (at time t=0t=0t=0). It is also guaranteed that PPP and QQQ do not touch the sphere at any time.

Find the smallest positive time tvt_vtv​ when QQQ is visible from PPP, i.e. when the line segment connecting points PPP and QQQ does not intersect the sphere.
constraints: The absolute values of coordinates of all points do not exceed 2*10^9
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first and only line of each test case contains 13 space-separated integers. The first three integers Px, Py, Pz denote the coordinates of point P. The next three integers Qx, Qy, Qz denote the initial coordinates of point Q. The next three integers dx, dy, dz denote the components of the direction vector d. The last four integers cx, cy, cz, r denote the coordinates of the centre of the sphere and its radius.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number — the time tv when Q is visible from P.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The line segment connecting P and Q does not intersect the sphere at time t = 1.

solution.approach: To find the smallest positive time tv when Q is visible from P, we need to find the time when the line segment connecting P and Q does not intersect the sphere. We can use the equation of the line segment and the equation of the sphere to find the intersection points. If the line segment does not intersect the sphere, we can find the time when Q is visible from P by finding the time when the distance between P and Q is greater than the radius of the sphere.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — per test case is O(1), total is O(T)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the coordinates and the radius

code.Python3:
import math
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    Px, Py, Pz, Qx, Qy, Qz, dx, dy, dz, cx, cy, cz, r = map(float, input().split())
    a = dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
    b = 2 * (dx * (Qx - cx) + dy * (Qy - cy) + dz * (Qz - cz))
    c = (Qx - cx) * (Qx - cx) + (Qy - cy) * (Qy - cy) + (Qz - cz) * (Qz - cz) - r * r
    D = b * b - 4 * a * c
    if D >= 0:
        t = (-b - math.sqrt(D)) / (2 * a)
        if t > 0:
            print(f"{t:.10f}")
        else:
            print(f"{(-b + math.sqrt(D)) / (2 * a):.10f}")
